properties.titulo_evento: European firms plan exo-atmospheric interceptor for 2027 test
properties.descricao: Bliksem EXO is meant to defeat ballistic threats including Russia’s intermediate-range Oreshnik missile with separating and maneuvering re-entry vehicles.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine agrees on plan to acquire 16 Rafale jets, France’s Macron says
properties.descricao: Ukraine has agreed on a plan to acquire 16 Rafale fighter jets from France together with the accompanying weapon systems, along with air-defense equipment.

properties.descricao: Ballistic missiles are the one threat Ukraine can't stop with a weapon it makes itself.

properties.descricao: Lawmakers demanded Monday that the Pentagon release its findings from an investigation into the Feb. 28 strike at a girls’ school in Iran.

properties.descricao: U.S. Central Command utilized three Corsair unmanned surface vessels to strike a submarine and ship maintenance facility in Iran.

properties.titulo_evento: Gibraltar border controls lifted: Is it part of Schengen, the UK – or both?
properties.descricao: A new treaty signed by Britain and the EU includes the removal of border checks between Spain and Gibraltar.

properties.titulo_evento: Crowds cross between Spain and Gibraltar following UK-EU treaty
properties.descricao: A large crowd crossed between Spain and Gibraltar in the early hours of Wednesday morning, celebrating the UK-EU treaty.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran launches attacks across region as US strikes continue
properties.descricao: Iran attacked Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan on Tuesday night, claiming to destroy the US Fifth Fleet’s command centre.

properties.titulo_evento: Russian attack on Odesa kills three as Ukraine targets vessels in Black Sea
properties.descricao: At least three people killed and three others wounded in Russian strikes on Odesa, city's military administrator says.

properties.titulo_evento: US resumes Iran ports blockade as Gulf attacks continue: What’s the latest?
properties.descricao: Less than a month after signing an MoU as a step towards peace, the US reinstates a naval blockade on Iranian ports.

properties.descricao: Oil prices rise as the US renews a blockade on Iranian ports and carries out a wave of air strikes.

properties.titulo_evento: Photos: Spain ends Mbappe and France’s World Cup dream to reach final
properties.descricao: France’s free-scoring World Cup run ends in 2-0 loss to Spain, denting Mbappe’s hopes of a third final.

properties.titulo_evento: Spain recapture 2010 spirit after reaching World Cup final: De la Fuente
properties.descricao: After a masterclass against France in the semifinals, Spain are just one win from winning their second World Cup.

properties.titulo_evento: How Iran’s southern coast is bearing the brunt of US attacks
properties.descricao: Donald Trump says the US is going to bomb Iran’s civilian infrastructure if there is no deal by next week.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran threatens to block more trade routes as US launches fresh strikes
properties.descricao: US President Donald Trump vowed to strike Iran's bridges and power plants next week if the country does not return to talks.

properties.descricao: The abrupt about-face from the US president was the latest twist in a conflict that has now lasted more than four months.

properties.titulo_evento: China economic growth falls sharply, missing target
properties.descricao: Weak demand domestically and the impact of the Iran war on oil prices overshadowed the country's strong exports.

properties.titulo_evento: Seven Britons among those killed in Spain wildfires
properties.descricao: Twelve of the 13 victims were foreign nationals, Spanish authorities said on Tuesday.

properties.titulo_evento: China detains US scientist who studied North Korea nuclear tests
properties.descricao: Chen Youlin's family says he has been wrongfully detained for nearly two years on spying charges.

properties.titulo_evento: Israeli strike on police post in north Gaza kills seven, officials say
properties.descricao: A senior officer in the Hamas-run police force was among those killed in the strike, which Israel's military says targeted "terrorists".

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine and EU agree joint drone production deal as Ursula von der Leyen visits Kyiv. Follow DW
properties.descricao: Ukraine and the EU have agreed on a drone deal combining Kyiv’s know-how with the bloc's scale-up power. Meanwhile, Russian attacks caused more fatalities in Odesa as Moscow increasingly targets the port. DW has more.

properties.titulo_evento: India news: Landmark trade deal with UK takes effect
properties.descricao: The free trade agreement allows zero-duty market access for nearly 99% of India's exports to the UK. Meanwhile, US senators have proposed a new bill to impose 100% tariffs on India for buying oil from Russia.

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war: US launches fresh wave of strikes

properties.titulo_evento: US troop withdrawal from Iraq set for September 30
properties.descricao: The planned US withdrawal is linked to the disarmament of influential Iran-backed militias in Iraq.

properties.titulo_evento: US-Iraq relations: Can Iran-allied militias be disarmed?
properties.descricao: When Donald Trump meets Iraq's new PM today, they are likely to discuss disarming Iran's allies in Iraq. Some have already pledged to give up their guns. But is that likely? And will it influence the US-Iran war?

properties.titulo_evento: Iran war: US launches new round of strikes
properties.descricao: The US military said the strikes were "to continue degrading Iranian capabilities." Iran meanwhile has said the US renewed blockade "dismantles" the truce deal.

properties.titulo_evento: As the world rearms, South Korea aims to cash in
properties.descricao: Weapons and military equipment produced in South Korea are sought-after due to their reliability, speedy delivery times and relatively low prices. Global demand for weapons has surged after Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany set to restrict its Freedom of Information Act
properties.descricao: Critics fear the reforms could weaken government transparency by curbing civil society's access to official information and limiting requests from foreigners.
